acceptanceCriteria: Case-insensitive on both tag name and attribute name (consistent with existing getTagDef behavior)
Returns the matching SsmlAttrDef when found; returns undefined when the tag is unknown or the attribute does not exist on that tag
Unit tests in apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts cover: a known attribute (e.g., break/time), a case-insensitive match (e.g., BREAK/TIME), an unknown tag, and a known tag with an unknown attribute
npm run test passes with no regressions
outOfScope: Changes to existing tag definitions, SsmlAttrDef type, or any module other than tags.ts and tags.test.ts
Performance optimization or caching beyond the simple lookup pattern already used by getTagDef
problemStatement: The SSML tag registry provides case-insensitive tag lookup (getTagDef) but has no equivalent for resolving an individual attribute definition within a tag. Every call site that needs a specific attribute — such as break's time or say-as's interpret-as — must hand-walk the attributes array with its own case-folding logic, leading to scattered, inconsistent, and error-prone access patterns across the codebase.
userValue: Developers building SSML tooling (validator, toolbar, inspectors) no longer need to manually scan def.attributes arrays at every call site. A single, consistent lookup function reduces duplication, eliminates off-by-one errors and case-sensitivity bugs, and mirrors the existing getTagDef pattern developers already know and trust.
